title: The Reason I Jump
author: Naoki Higashida
asd_themes: non-verbal communication sensory inner experience autism understanding behaviour
description: A thirteen-year-old boy with severe autism answers questions about his inner world. Provides rare insight into the sensory and emotional experience of a non-verbal child with ASD — essential reading for any parent navigating early communication delays.

title: Ten Things Every Child with Autism Wishes You Knew
author: Ellen Notbohm
asd_themes: sensory communication social skills behaviour understanding child autism parent guide
description: A compassionate, practical guide that translates the autistic child's perspective for parents and educators. Covers sensory sensitivities, communication challenges, social interaction, and building connection — directly aligned with Q-CHAT-10 developmental areas.

title: Uniquely Human: A Different Way of Seeing Autism
author: Barry M. Prizant
asd_themes: autism behaviour communication social emotional regulation intervention speech
description: Reframes autism as a different way of experiencing the world rather than a disorder to be fixed. Offers practical strategies grounded in decades of clinical work — particularly useful for parents whose toddlers show communication and social interaction delays.

title: An Early Start for Your Child with Autism
author: Sally J. Rogers, Geraldine Dawson, Laurie Vismara
asd_themes: early intervention toddler play social communication imitation learning autism speech
description: Based on the ESDM (Early Start Denver Model), the gold-standard early intervention programme for toddlers with ASD. Packed with hands-on activities that build communication, play, social interaction, and cognitive skills — perfectly matched to the Q-CHAT-10 screening areas.

title: Autism Spectrum Disorders: Intervention Strategies (ERIC Report)
author: US Dept of Education / ERIC
asd_themes: autism spectrum intervention speech language therapy early childhood education
description: A publicly available research synthesis on evidence-based intervention strategies for children with ASD, covering speech-language therapy, applied behaviour analysis, and social skills training for toddlers and young children.

title: Understanding Autism Spectrum Disorder (OpenStax / BCcampus)
author: BCcampus Open Education
asd_themes: autism spectrum disorder understanding diagnosis behaviour sensory social communication
description: A freely downloadable open textbook covering the neuroscience, diagnosis, and intervention landscape of ASD. Written for non-specialists and includes chapters on early signs, sensory processing, communication support, and family strategies.

title: The Out-of-Sync Child
author: Carol Stock Kranowitz
asd_themes: sensory processing disorder sensory integration meltdowns routine calm behaviour
description: The definitive parent guide to Sensory Processing Disorder, which co-occurs frequently with ASD. Explains why children overreact or underreact to sensory input and provides practical home strategies — directly relevant for toddlers flagging Q-CHAT items A9 and A10.

title: Engaging Autism: Using the Floortime Approach
author: Stanley I. Greenspan, Serena Wieder
asd_themes: play social interaction eye contact joint attention engagement floortime autism toddler
description: Presents the DIR/Floortime model — a play-based intervention where parents follow the child's lead to build social connection, joint attention, and communication. Particularly effective for toddlers with limited eye contact, play deficits, and low social engagement.

title: More Than Words (Hanen Programme)
author: Fern Sussman
asd_themes: speech delay language non-verbal communication talk words toddler autism parent
description: The companion book to the Hanen 'More Than Words' programme — the most widely used parent-implemented communication intervention for young children with ASD. Practical, illustrated strategies for building functional communication with non-verbal or minimally verbal toddlers.

title: Early Identification of Autism Spectrum Disorder (WHO Guide)
author: World Health Organization
asd_themes: autism early identification screening toddler diagnosis social communication behaviour
description: A freely available WHO guidance document on the early identification of ASD in low- and middle-income contexts. Covers red flags, validated screening tools (including Q-CHAT), and referral pathways — useful for understanding the clinical basis of this screening tool.

title: A Parent's Guide to Autism (Autism Speaks)
author: Autism Speaks
asd_themes: autism parent guide diagnosis intervention therapy social communication sensory
description: A comprehensive, freely downloadable guide covering everything from first concerns through diagnosis, early intervention options, navigating therapies, and building a support network. Covers ABA, speech therapy, occupational therapy, and social skills programmes.

title: Teach Me Language
author: Sabrina Freeman, Lorelei Dake
asd_themes: language learning speech communication words social understanding autism therapy
description: A structured language programme designed for children with autism who have some speech but struggle with grammar, conversation, and social language. Provides hundreds of reproducible exercises targeting the exact verbal communication delays flagged in Q-CHAT items A3, A4, and A7.

title: Visual Supports for People with Autism
author: Marlene J. Cohen, Donna L. Sloan
asd_themes: visual supports routine calm sensory meltdowns behaviour repetitive autism toddler
description: A practical manual for creating and implementing visual schedules, social stories, and environmental supports for children with ASD. Directly addresses the sensory regulation and routine-dependence that underlie Q-CHAT items A9 and A10.
